root_text.an4.191:1.1: “Sotānugatānaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammānaṁ, vacasā paricitānaṁ, manasānupekkhitānaṁ, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhānaṁ cattāro ānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā.
root_text.an4.191:1.4: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.191:1.5: Tassa te dhammā sotānugatā honti, vacasā paricitā, manasānupekkhitā, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an4.191:1.6: So muṭṭhassati kālaṁ kurumāno aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.191:1.9: atha so satto khippaṁyeva visesagāmī hoti.
root_text.an4.191:1.10: Sotānugatānaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammānaṁ, vacasā paricitānaṁ, manasānupekkhitānaṁ, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhānaṁ ayaṁ paṭhamo ānisaṁso pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.191:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.191:2.2: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.191:2.3: Tassa te dhammā sotānugatā honti, vacasā paricitā, manasānupekkhitā, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an4.191:2.4: So muṭṭhassati kālaṁ kurumāno aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.191:2.5: Tassa tattha na heva kho sukhino dhammapadā plavanti;
root_text.an4.191:2.6: api ca kho bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.an4.191:2.8: ‘ayaṁ vā so dhammavinayo, yatthāhaṁ pubbe brahmacariyaṁ acarin’ti.
root_text.an4.191:2.10: atha so satto khippameva visesagāmī hoti.
root_text.an4.191:2.13: Tassa na heva kho assa kaṅkhā vā vimati vā:
root_text.an4.191:2.14: ‘bherisaddo nu kho, na nu kho bherisaddo’ti.
root_text.an4.191:2.16: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.191:2.17: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.191:2.18: Tassa te dhammā sotānugatā honti, vacasā paricitā, manasānupekkhitā, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an4.191:2.19: So muṭṭhassati kālaṁ kurumāno aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.191:2.20: Tassa tattha na heva kho sukhino dhammapadā plavanti;
root_text.an4.191:2.21: api ca kho bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.an4.191:2.23: ‘ayaṁ vā so dhammavinayo, yatthāhaṁ pubbe brahmacariyaṁ acarin’ti.
root_text.an4.191:2.25: atha so satto khippaṁyeva visesagāmī hoti.
root_text.an4.191:2.26: Sotānugatānaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammānaṁ, vacasā paricitānaṁ, manasānupekkhitānaṁ, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhānaṁ ayaṁ dutiyo ānisaṁso pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.191:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.191:3.2: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.191:3.3: Tassa te dhammā sotānugatā honti, vacasā paricitā, manasānupekkhitā, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an4.191:3.4: So muṭṭhassati kālaṁ kurumāno aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.191:3.5: Tassa tattha na heva kho sukhino dhammapadā plavanti, napi bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti;
root_text.an4.191:3.6: api ca kho devaputto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.an4.191:3.8: ‘ayaṁ vā so dhammavinayo, yatthāhaṁ pubbe brahmacariyaṁ acarin’ti.
root_text.an4.191:3.10: atha so satto khippaṁyeva visesagāmī hoti.
root_text.an4.191:3.13: Tassa na heva kho assa kaṅkhā vā vimati vā:
root_text.an4.191:3.14: ‘saṅkhasaddo nu kho, na nu kho saṅkhasaddo’ti.
root_text.an4.191:3.16: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.191:3.17: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.191:3.18: Tassa te dhammā sotānugatā honti, vacasā paricitā, manasānupekkhitā, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an4.191:3.19: So muṭṭhassati kālaṁ kurumāno aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.191:3.20: Tassa tattha na heva kho sukhino dhammapadā plavanti, napi bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti;
root_text.an4.191:3.21: api ca kho devaputto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti.
root_text.an4.191:3.23: ‘ayaṁ vā so dhammavinayo, yatthāhaṁ pubbe brahmacariyaṁ acarin’ti.
root_text.an4.191:3.25: atha so satto khippaṁyeva visesagāmī hoti.
root_text.an4.191:3.26: Sotānugatānaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammānaṁ, vacasā paricitānaṁ, manasānupekkhitānaṁ, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhānaṁ ayaṁ tatiyo ānisaṁso pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.191:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.191:4.2: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.191:4.3: Tassa te dhammā sotānugatā honti, vacasā paricitā, manasānupekkhitā, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an4.191:4.4: So muṭṭhassati kālaṁ kurumāno aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.191:4.5: Tassa tattha na heva kho sukhino dhammapadā plavanti, napi bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti, napi devaputto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti;
root_text.an4.191:4.6: api ca kho opapātiko opapātikaṁ sāreti:
root_text.an4.191:4.7: ‘sarasi tvaṁ, mārisa, sarasi tvaṁ, mārisa, yattha mayaṁ pubbe brahmacariyaṁ acarimhā’ti.
root_text.an4.191:4.11: atha so satto khippaṁyeva visesagāmī hoti.
root_text.an4.191:4.14: Añño pana sahāyako sahāyakaṁ evaṁ vadeyya:
root_text.an4.191:4.18: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu dhammaṁ pariyāpuṇāti—
root_text.an4.191:4.19: suttaṁ, geyyaṁ, veyyākaraṇaṁ, gāthaṁ, udānaṁ, itivuttakaṁ, jātakaṁ, abbhutadhammaṁ, vedallaṁ.
root_text.an4.191:4.20: Tassa te dhammā sotānugatā honti, vacasā paricitā, manasānupekkhitā, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an4.191:4.21: So muṭṭhassati kālaṁ kurumāno aññataraṁ devanikāyaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an4.191:4.22: Tassa tattha na heva kho sukhino dhammapadā plavanti, napi bhikkhu iddhimā cetovasippatto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti, napi devaputto devaparisāyaṁ dhammaṁ deseti;
root_text.an4.191:4.23: api ca kho opapātiko opapātikaṁ sāreti:
root_text.an4.191:4.24: ‘sarasi tvaṁ, mārisa, sarasi tvaṁ, mārisa, yattha mayaṁ pubbe brahmacariyaṁ acarimhā’ti.
root_text.an4.191:4.28: atha kho so satto khippaṁyeva visesagāmī hoti.
root_text.an4.191:4.29: Sotānugatānaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammānaṁ, vacasā paricitānaṁ, manasānupekkhitānaṁ, diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhānaṁ ayaṁ catuttho ānisaṁso pāṭikaṅkho.
root_text.an4.191:4.30: Sotānugatānaṁ, bhikkhave, dhammānaṁ, vacasā paricitānaṁ, manasānupekkhitānaṁ diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhānaṁ ime cattāro ānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā”ti.

translation_text.an4.191:1.1: “Mendicants, you can expect four benefits when the teachings have been followed by ear, consolidated by voice, examined by the mind, and well comprehended theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:1.3: Take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an4.191:1.4: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an4.191:1.5: They’ve followed those teachings by ear, consolidated them by voice, examined them by the mind, and well comprehended them theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:1.6: But they die unmindful and are reborn in one of the orders of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:1.7: Being happy there, passages of the teaching come back to them.
translation_text.an4.191:1.9: but then that being quickly reaches distinction.
translation_text.an4.191:1.10: This is the first benefit you can expect when the teachings have been followed by ear, consolidated by voice, examined by the mind, and well comprehended theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:2.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an4.191:2.2: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an4.191:2.3: They’ve followed those teachings by ear, consolidated them by voice, examined them by the mind, and well comprehended them theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:2.4: But they die unmindful and are reborn in one of the orders of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:2.5: Though they’re happy there, passages of the teaching don’t come back to them.
translation_text.an4.191:2.6: However, a mendicant with psychic powers, who has achieved mastery of the mind, teaches Dhamma to the assembly of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:2.8: ‘I used to lead the spiritual life in this same teaching and training.’
translation_text.an4.191:2.10: but then that being quickly reaches distinction.
translation_text.an4.191:2.11: Suppose a person was skilled in the sound of drums.
translation_text.an4.191:2.12: While traveling along a road they hear the sound of drums.
translation_text.an4.191:2.13: They wouldn’t have any doubts or uncertainties about whether
translation_text.an4.191:2.14: that was the sound of drums or not.
translation_text.an4.191:2.15: They’d just conclude, ‘That’s the sound of drums.’
translation_text.an4.191:2.16: In the same way, take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching …
translation_text.an4.191:2.19: But they die unmindful and are reborn in one of the orders of gods. …
translation_text.an4.191:2.25: but then that being quickly reaches distinction.
translation_text.an4.191:2.26: This is the second benefit you can expect when the teachings have been followed by ear, consolidated by voice, examined by the mind, and well comprehended theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:3.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an4.191:3.2: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an4.191:3.3: They’ve followed those teachings by ear, consolidated them by voice, examined them by the mind, and well comprehended them theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:3.4: But they die unmindful and are reborn in one of the orders of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:3.5: But passages of the teaching don’t come back to them when they’re happy, nor does a mendicant with psychic powers … teach Dhamma to the assembly of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:3.6: However, a godling teaches Dhamma to the assembly of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:3.8: ‘I used to lead the spiritual life in this same teaching and training.’
translation_text.an4.191:3.10: but then that being quickly reaches distinction.
translation_text.an4.191:3.11: Suppose a person was skilled in the sound of horns.
translation_text.an4.191:3.12: While traveling along a road they hear the sound of horns.
translation_text.an4.191:3.13: They wouldn’t have any doubt about whether
translation_text.an4.191:3.14: that was the sound of horns or not.
translation_text.an4.191:3.15: They’d just conclude, ‘That’s the sound of horns.’
translation_text.an4.191:3.16: In the same way, take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching …
translation_text.an4.191:3.19: But they die unmindful and are reborn in one of the orders of gods. …
translation_text.an4.191:3.25: but then that being quickly reaches distinction.
translation_text.an4.191:3.26: This is the third benefit you can expect when the teachings have been followed by ear, consolidated by voice, examined by the mind, and well comprehended theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:4.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching—
translation_text.an4.191:4.2: statements, mixed prose & verse, discussions, verses, inspired exclamations, legends, stories of past lives, amazing stories, and elaborations.
translation_text.an4.191:4.3: They’ve followed those teachings by ear, consolidated them by voice, examined them by the mind, and well comprehended them theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:4.4: But they die unmindful and are reborn in one of the orders of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:4.5: But passages of the teaching don’t come back to them when they’re happy, and neither a mendicant with psychic powers … nor a godling teaches Dhamma to the assembly of gods.
translation_text.an4.191:4.6: But a being who has been reborn spontaneously reminds another such being:
translation_text.an4.191:4.7: ‘Do you remember, good fellow? Do you remember where we used to lead the spiritual life?’
translation_text.an4.191:4.11: but then that being quickly reaches distinction.
translation_text.an4.191:4.12: Suppose there were two friends who had played together in the sand.
translation_text.an4.191:4.13: Some time or other they’d meet.
translation_text.an4.191:4.14: And one friend would say to the other:
translation_text.an4.191:4.15: ‘Do you remember this, dear? Do you remember that, dear?’
translation_text.an4.191:4.18: In the same way, take a mendicant who memorizes the teaching …
translation_text.an4.191:4.21: But they die unmindful and are reborn in one of the orders of gods. …
translation_text.an4.191:4.28: but then that being quickly reaches distinction.
translation_text.an4.191:4.29: This is the fourth benefit you can expect when the teachings have been followed by ear, consolidated by voice, examined by the mind, and well comprehended theoretically.
translation_text.an4.191:4.30: You can expect these four benefits when the teachings have been followed by ear, consolidated by voice, examined by the mind, and well comprehended theoretically.”

variant_text.an4.191:4.13: samāgaccheyyuṁ → samāgaccheyyuṁ, tamenaṁ (si, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
